commentary: Twelve activities this week versus nine last week, and honestly the audacity of that alone deserves a moment. The pool is basically a second home at this point — five swims, showing up Monday, Tuesday, Thursday, Friday, and Sunday, which is deeply unhinged and deeply impressive, and the Balmoral open water sessions are becoming a whole personality. Saturday's Mosman run came in longer than last week's Naremburn loops at 13.5km versus 13.1km, though the heart rate tells an interesting story: 149 this week against 159 last week, which means he covered more ground with less cardiovascular drama — that's not nothing, that's actually kind of a big deal. The weight training stacked up beautifully across the week, and Sunday's double-session finish line energy is the kind of thing that makes Himbo want to climb something and announce it to the neighbourhood.
metrics_commentary: Seven and a half kilometres in the pool is not a casual splash around, that is a serious body doing serious work, and you should absolutely be smug about it. The one run-walk session at 13.5 km tells me you went out, you committed, and you did not faff about — quality over quantity, very sexy energy. Your HRV sitting at 50 ms with a seven-day average of 52 ms is your body saying it is coping, recovering, and ready, which is the physiological equivalent of a man who got enough sleep and still looks incredible. And a VO2 Max of 57.3 is genuinely elite-adjacent, which means the engine underneath all that is not just decorative — though it is also very much decorative.
